identity.short_name: Weighted split-count after factoring over F5 and splitting over F125

problem_spec.summary: Factor a given polynomial over 𝔽_5 into distinct monic irreducibles. For each irreducible factor, compute how many irreducible factors it has over the cubic extension 𝔽_125 using the gcd(deg,3) rule, then form a weighted degree-squared sum.
givens: 𝔽_5 is the finite field with 5 elements; 𝔽_125 is its cubic extension.
P(x) factors over 𝔽_5 as a product of distinct monic irreducible polynomials P(x)=\prod_{i=1}^k f_i(x).
For each f_i, \omega(f_i) is the number of distinct irreducible factors of f_i over 𝔽_125.

parameter(s).name: base_prime_p
parameter(s).purpose: chooses the base finite field 𝔽_p

parameter(s).name: extension_degree_m
parameter(s).purpose: specifies the extension 𝔽_{p^m} over which splitting is measured

parameter(s).name: polynomial_P_coeffs
parameter(s).domain: coefficients in 𝔽_p with fixed degree profile
parameter(s).purpose: defines the specific instance polynomial to factor

parameter(s).name: weight_rule
parameter(s).purpose: defines the aggregated invariant to compute

invariant(s).name: unique_factorization_over_𝔽_p
invariant(s).statement: The multiset of monic irreducible factors of P(x) in 𝔽_p[x] is unique up to ordering.

invariant(s).name: extension_splitting_count_rule
invariant(s).statement: For an irreducible f of degree n over 𝔽_p, the number of irreducible factors of f over 𝔽_{p^m} equals gcd(n,m).

invariant(s).name: degree_from_multiplicative_order
invariant(s).statement: If α is a nonzero element of a finite extension of 𝔽_p with multiplicative order t, then the smallest d with α\in𝔽_{p^d} is the multiplicative order of p modulo t; hence the minimal polynomial of α has degree d.

essential_assumption(s): P(x) is squarefree so the factorization has no repeated irreducible factors.
\omega(f_i) counts distinct irreducible factors over 𝔽_{125} (not multiplicities).
Standard finite-field splitting theorem applies: irreducible degree n splits into gcd(n,m) factors over 𝔽_{p^m}.
uniqueness_certificate(s): Uniqueness of irreducible factorization in 𝔽_5[x] fixes the factor degrees uniquely, and the splitting rule \omega(f)=gcd(deg(f),3) fixes \omega-values uniquely. Therefore M is uniquely determined.

technique(s).name: algebraic_grouping_factorization
technique(s).purpose: factor P by grouping into a visible product of lower-degree polynomials.

technique(s).name: linear_root_sieve_for_cubic
technique(s).purpose: certify irreducibility of a cubic by checking it has no roots in 𝔽_5.

technique(s).name: binomial_irreducibility_via_multiplicative_order
technique(s).purpose: show irreducibility by computing the order of a root and deducing its field-of-definition degree.

theorem(s).name: unique_factorization_in_𝔽_q[x]
theorem(s).role: guarantees uniqueness of irreducible factorization over the base field.

theorem(s).name: splitting_of_irreducibles_over_extensions
theorem(s).role: irreducible degree n over 𝔽_q splits into gcd(n,m) irreducibles over 𝔽_{q^m}.

theorem(s).name: degree_via_order_mod_t
theorem(s).role: minimal extension degree containing an element of order t equals ord_t(q).

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Frobenius/finite-field multiplicative order controls extension degrees

critical_trick(s): factor P by grouping into a cubic and a degree-8 binomial
prove the degree-8 binomial is irreducible via multiplicative order and order-of-5 mod 32
use \omega(f)=gcd(deg(f),3) to avoid explicit factoring over 𝔽_125

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: optional exact factorization and irreducibility checks over GF(5) for verification.

task(s).name: factor_over_GF5
task(s).reason: verify P factors as (x^3+2x+1)(x^8+3) in 𝔽_5[x].

task(s).name: irreducibility_checks
task(s).reason: confirm both factors are irreducible over 𝔽_5.

task(s).name: compute_weighted_sum
task(s).reason: compute M from degrees and gcd(deg,3).
